title: hwid buffer not zeroed after use in generate_eui64
description:
lora_l2.c:90 allocates hwid[32] to hold hardware device ID. The buffer is populated at line 90 and hashed at line 105, but never zeroed before function return.

What goes wrong: The hardware ID persists on stack after function returns. While not secret (it's hardware-derived), this is inconsistent with the pattern established at line 131 where hash is zeroed. A contributor adding sensitive data to this function might assume stack cleanup is automatic.

Also inconsistent with the secure_zero pattern used elsewhere for sensitive buffers.

Fix: Add secure_zero(hwid, sizeof(hwid)) in the cleanup section alongside the hash zeroing.
close_reason: Fixed: Added secure_zero(hwid, sizeof(hwid)) in cleanup section at line 132, consistent with existing pattern for hash buffer zeroing
